title: Koenigia × fennica

It is often employed in folk remedies to treat various conditions, including infections and skin irritations. taxonomy: Koenigia × fennica is a hybrid species of flowering plant, specifically a cross between Koenigia lanata and Koenigia polystachya. It is classified under the family Polygonaceae. As a hybrid, Koenigia × fennica is not assigned a specific species rank in the traditional sense, but rather is recognized as a distinct entity within the larger Koenigia genus. intro: Koenigia × fennica, also known as the Fennoscandian Koenigia, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Arctic tundra regions of Norway and Sweden. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Koenigia × fennica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat respiratory and cardiovascular conditions.
